Story: As a user, I want the simulation to start in an interactive state immediately, so I can observe emergent behavior without setup delay.
As a user, I want a sensible seeded/default initial world, so I can begin experimenting right away.
As a user, I want to pause and resume simulation time instantly, so I can inspect states and continue from the same context.
As a user, I want to control particle/agent counts, so I can tune complexity, interaction frequency, and performance.
As a user, I want to configure the number and composition of species/types/charges, so I can test different diversity regimes.
As a user, I want to rebalance type distributions (including equalization), so I can run fair behavior comparisons.
As a user, I want to switch interaction-rule presets quickly, so I can explore known behavior families.
As a user, I want to edit interaction values directly (including matrix-level controls), so I can run precise custom experiments.
As a user, I want physically meaningful attraction/repulsion/collision rules, so emergent structures remain believable.
As a user, I want to pick templates/presets before placement, so I can initialize scenarios quickly.
As a user, I want to define and reuse custom templates, so I can iterate on design patterns efficiently.
As a user, I want to add, move, drag, brush, and delete entities directly in the world, so I can sculpt scenarios in real time.
As a user, I want continuous placement and disturbance tools (e.g., zap), so I can test resilience and dynamic response.
As a user, I want clear selection/deselection targeting, so I can modify only intended entities.
As a user, I want cursor visibility/size/shape controls, so targeting stays accurate across tasks and scales.
As a user, I want smooth pan/zoom/navigation and quick reset/focus actions, so I can inspect both local detail and global structure.
As a user, I want control over palette/shader/size/trails/overlays, so patterns remain readable across densities and themes.
As a user, I want context overlays (like influence ranges) during parameter edits, so I can understand visual impact immediately.
As a user, I want live controls for key simulation parameters, so I can explore outcomes without restarting or editing code.
As a user, I want boundary-mode and timestep controls, so I can test dynamics under different integration constraints.
As a user, I want bounded velocities/damping safeguards, so runs stay stable instead of exploding.
As a user, I want to tune compute parallelism (e.g., thread count), so I can match available hardware.
As a user, I want responsive UI while simulation runs, so interaction remains fluid under load.
As a user, I want telemetry like FPS and entity counts, so I can judge scaling and runtime health.
As a user, I want my app preferences remembered across sessions, so I do not repeat setup every launch.
As a user, I want to save and reload named states, so I can revisit and share exact scenarios.
As a user, I want reset/reseed controls, so I can reproduce runs and compare outcomes consistently.
As a user, I want searchable save browsing with visual previews and delete management, so I can maintain a usable experiment library.
As a user, I want in-context onboarding and visible control hints, so I can become productive quickly.
As a user, I want an always-available shortcuts/control reference, so I can learn and recall interactions while working.
As a user, I want structured learning paths with guided examples, so I can progress from basics to advanced concepts.
As a user, I want curated references and clean navigation between simulation and learning content, so study and practice stay connected.
As a user, I want controls and layouts to work across screen sizes and input modes, so the experience remains usable on desktop and mobile.
As a user, I want actionable runtime errors with copyable diagnostics, so I can report and resolve issues quickly.
As a user, I want automatic stall detection and safe recovery paths, so I can continue work without full restarts.
As a user, I want cross-platform compatibility with stable dependencies, so the app runs consistently across environments.
As a user, I want reproducible packaged builds (including Windows), so releases are dependable and repeatable.
As a user, I want UI panel interactions to be isolated from world input, so adjusting controls never causes accidental world edits.
